title: OMV Receives EUR 123 Mn in Funding for the Largest Green Hydrogen Project in Austria
content: OMV has secured up to EUR 123 million in production funding from Austria Wirtschaftsservice GmbH (aws) for a new 140 MW green hydrogen plant in Bruck an der Leitha. Supported by a positive assessment from the European Hydrogen Bank, the project aims to commence operations by the end of 2027. It will produce up to 23,000 tons of green hydrogen annually using renewable energy, transporting it via a 22-kilometer pipeline to decarbonize the Schwechat refinery. This is expected to cut carbon emissions by 150,000 tons per year. OMV CEO Alfred Stern highlighted the project as a milestone for Europe's energy transition. The plant will be developed and operated through a joint venture with Masdar, which is expected to be finalized in early 2026.
author: Analysts Account
